page: Microsoft Azure NC24rs_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCSv3 server family. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 Intel processor architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 448.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 18.67 GB per vCPU. Additionally, the server integrates four hardware GPUs and includes 3019 GB of local storage.

This server configuration supports Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) networking and is Premium Storage capable, enabling high-throughput and low-latency interconnectivity for clustered deployments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of substantial system memory, local storage, and multiple GPUs positions this instance as a high-density resource allocation suited for workloads requiring significant parallel processing capabilities and large memory footprints.

Given the hardware profile, the NC24rs_v3 is designed for demanding workloads such as GPU-accelerated deep learning, high-performance computing simulations, large-scale data processing, and clustered parallel database workloads that leverage RDMA capabilities.
description: Microsoft Azure NC24rs_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCSv3 family, designed for high-performance computing and parallel processing workloads. It features 24 dedicated x86_64 Intel vCPUs, 448.0 GB of system memory, four GPUs, and 3019 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 18.67 GB per vCPU. The server is RDMA-capable and Premium Storage capable, facilitating low-latency clustered networking and high-speed storage access. With dedicated CPU allocation, it ensures predictable execution without resource sharing. This hardware profile is suited for deep learning, scientific simulations, and memory-intensive parallel computing tasks.
